name: Asymmetric signing, JWK export & OIDC error types
outcome: JwtService can sign and verify tokens with a consumer-supplied RSA keypair and expose the public key as a JWK set; the existing symmetric path is untouched except that verify() now pins the configured algorithm; OIDC's invalid_token and insufficient_scope error types exist for downstream issues to consume.
scope: Extend JwtService for asymmetric RSA keys (RS256 only — ES256 is deferred to v2 per the OIDC Discovery §3 RS256-mandatory requirement and the single-key model), fix sign() to forward SignOptions including a header override, pin the algorithm on verify(), add an optional JWKS-exposing method to JwtInterface, and add the invalid_token / insufficient_scope error types to OAuthException (moved here from later issues to avoid an OAuthException merge conflict between issues 4 and 6). No endpoints, no OIDC config yet.
acceptance_criteria: A JwtService built with an RSA private key signs and verifies RS256 tokens; ES256 (and any non-RS256 algorithm) is explicitly rejected at construction in v1
The JWT header includes a kid; kid defaults to the RFC 7638 thumbprint and can be overridden
getKeySet returns a valid JWK set containing the public key with use:"sig", alg:"RS256", a kid matching the token header, and NO private members (d, p, q, dp, dq, qi)
The RFC 7638 thumbprint passes the OpenID Foundation known-answer test vector (the JWK in RFC 7638 §3.1 hashing to NzbLsXh8uDCcd-6MNwXF4W_7noWXFZAfHkxZsRGC9Xs)
The existing string-secret HS256 path signs and verifies byte-identically to today: a snapshot assertion on the JOSE header confirms exactly {alg:HS256,typ:JWT} with no kid

verify() pins the algorithm — it always passes algorithms:[configuredAlgorithm], ignoring any caller-supplied algorithms VerifyOptions or token-supplied alg header. An algorithm-confusion attack token (HS256 signed using the RSA public key as the HMAC secret) is rejected; an alg:none token is rejected
sign() forwards a header override so a caller can set a custom typ such as at+jwt
Malformed inputs fail at construction with a clear error: garbage PEM, a public-only key (no private material), and a key whose type does not match the configured algorithm
OAuthException.invalidToken() and OAuthException.insufficientScope() static helpers exist and carry the correct OAuth 2.0 error codes (invalid_token, insufficient_scope) and HTTP status (401, 403)
steps: JwtService constructor — accept an asymmetric key option object ({ key, kid?, algorithm? }) alongside the existing Secret string. Derive the public key from the private key via Node crypto (createPublicKey). Reject non-RS256 algorithms at construction; reject public-only inputs
JwtService.sign — forward SignOptions (algorithm, keyid, header) to jsonwebtoken so RS256 tokens carry a kid header and a caller can set a custom typ (e.g. at+jwt)

JwtService — default kid to the RFC 7638 JWK thumbprint via a hand-rolled function: canonical JSON of the required JWK members (kty, n, e for RSA), SHA-256, base64url. Unit-test directly against the RFC 7638 §3.1 known-answer vector
JwtService.verify — pin the verification algorithm by always passing algorithms:[configuredAlgorithm], ignoring any caller-supplied or token-supplied algorithm (algorithm-confusion defense, ADR 0003)

JwtInterface — add an optional getKeySet method exposing the public key as a JWK set ({ keys: [{ kty, n, e, use:"sig", alg:"RS256", kid }] }); implement it on JwtService stripping any private members before export
OAuthException — add invalidToken() and insufficientScope() static helpers with the right error codes and HTTP statuses; place here (not issue 6) so issues 4 and 6 do not both edit OAuthException

test/unit — RS256 round trips; JWK export shape and no-private-members assertion; thumbprint against the RFC 7638 KAT; sign() header/typ forwarding; verify() algorithm pin via the HS256-with-RSA-public-key forged-token test and an alg:none test; malformed-key construction failures; the HS256 JOSE-header byte-identical regression snapshot
CHANGELOG — record that verify() now pins algorithms and a caller-supplied algorithms VerifyOptions is ignored; pointer to the upgrade-guide note added in issue 9
notes: Foundation slice — unblocks everything. JwtService.sign currently drops its options argument; fixing that is required here. No new dependency: jsonwebtoken plus Node crypto (createPublicKey, JWK export). The RFC 7638 thumbprint has no crypto primitive — it is a small hand-rolled function and is unit-tested against the spec's own KAT. Re-pointed 3 → 5 to reflect the crypto risk surface (sign/verify, thumbprint, public-key derivation, JWK export, algorithm pinning, malformed-input handling, plus the OAuthException additions). ES256 is deferred — see PRD out_of_scope. verify() now pins algorithms:['HS256'] for existing symmetric consumers too — a safe-by-default tightening that requires a CHANGELOG entry, not a token-format change. See docs/adr/0001 and docs/adr/0003.
